title: FFPE Post-Xenium Iterative Indirect Immunofluorescence Imaging (4i)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1hhnm","text":"Spatial transcriptomics (ST) enables high-plex, in situ gene expression profiling at subcellular resolution while preserving tissue architecture, offering powerful insights into tissue organization. However, current ST workflows are limited in resolving morphologically complex cell types when relying solely on RNA localization. Additionally, ST platforms do not directly capture pathological protein markers. To address these limitations, we developed an integrated post-spatial profiling workflow that combines the XeniumTM In Situ Gene Expression platform from 10x Genomics with iterative indirect immunofluorescence imaging (4i) on formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded (FFPE) post-mortem human brain tissue. This approach enables multiplexed, multi-omic mapping of the pathological microenvironment from the same tissue section, aligning spatial transcriptomic data with proteomic information. The use of FFPE tissue best preserves cellular morphology following Xenium processing and expands the range of markers available for multiplex staining. By incorporating cell-type-specific and neuropathological markers into a customizable 4i panel, this workflow improves transcript-to-cell assignment, provides spatially resolved insight into disease-associated cellular changes and interactions, and is adaptable for diverse approaches to downstream analyses.  ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4gi8g","text":"","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d7ibk","text":"In this protocol, we describe a 4i workflow for Xenium processed 5 μm FFPE human brain tissue sections, that can generate up to a 20-plex image. We outline key procedures for post-Xenium sample processing, antigen retrieval, recommended tissue washing to prevent microtears, and an optimized β-mercaptoethanol (βME)-based stripping solution for efficient antibody elution. ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d53d0","text":"","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cgnoa","text":"Additionally, we recommend following our related Xenium protocols from our Neuroimmunology Core profile, which provide best practices for tissue quality control and serial sectioning to ensure high quality staining performance post-processing.  ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"94s4q","text":"This protocol details an optimized workflow for 4i post-Xenium on FFPE human brain tissue, highlighting important sample processing tips, antigen retrieval recommendations, and an effective stripping solution for antibody elution.  ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"elvm3","text":"Post-Xenium Sample Processing ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":30}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f8hrg","text":"","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d2fuo","text":"Iterative indirect immunofluorescence (4i) staining was performed on 5 μm FFPE human brain tissue sections following Xenium processing. Post-Xenium tissue slides can be stored in 1X phosphate-buffered saline containing Tween-20 (PBS-T) at 4°C for up to 7 days, with recommended buffer changes every 2-3 days. Tissue sections were washed with 1X PBS-T prior to immunofluorescence staining.  ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"315tj","text":"Please follow proper chemical safety and disposal guidelines when handling βME and sharps. Always wear the appropriate personal protective equipment when performing this experiment.  ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Gently wash tissue with 1X PBS-T X3 We recommend using end-opening slide mailers to allow gentle, efficient washing and to prevent microtears or tissue detachment from the slide.
Retrieve Xenium slides stored in slide mailer with 1X PBS-T
Prepare 400 mL of 1X Citrate pH 6 antigen retrieval in Milli-Q H2O We prepare 360 mL of Milli-Q H2O in a clean 500 mL graduated cylinder and add up to 400 mL of 10X Citrate pH 6.
Microwave tissue in antigen retrieval at 400 W (30% power) for 25 min
Wash tissue with 1X PBS-T X3
Incubate tissue with 88% Formic Acid for 2 min (if applicable) When using Aβ markers, we recommend treating tissue with 88% formic acid and washing the slide with Milli-Q H2O.
Wash tissue with 1X PBS-T X3
Incubate tissue with primary antibodies in 1% NDS prepared in 1X PBS at 4°C overnight
Block tissue with 5% Normal Donkey Serum (NDS) for 30 min
Wash tissue with 1X PBS-T X3
Incubate tissue with secondary antibodies in 1% NDS/PBS for 1 hour

Incubate tissue with 1X TrueBlack for 2 min 1X TrueBlack was prepared by combining 70% ethanol and 20X TrueBlack. The solution can be stored long-term in a light-sensitive tube at room temperature (RT).
Incubate tissue with conjugated antibodies in 1% NDS/PBS for 1 hour (if applicable)
Block tissue with 5% NDS for 30 min (if applicable) We recommend blocking the tissue if a conjugated antibody is used in the staining cycle.
Wash tissue with 1X PBS-T X3
Wash tissue with 1X PBS-T X3
Mount tissue with Antifade ProLong Gold DAPI
Perform image acquisition to prepare for next round of staining
Coverslip slide and let dry overnight @ RT (long-term storage @ 4°C in slide box)
Prepare a sterile media bottle for βME stripping solution
Add 20 mL of 10% Sodium Dodecyl Sulfate (SDS)
Add 6.25 mL of 1M Tris-HCl pH 6.8
Microwave 400 mL of Milli-Q H2O at 100% power for 2 min The water temperature should be about 60°C.
Add up to 100 mL of microwaved Milli-Q H2O to media bottle
To confirm the stripping solution has a pH value of 6.8, use a pH meter and adjust as needed

Under the fume hood, add 800 μL of βME to the solution When working with βME, ensure proper personal protective equipment (PPE) is worn and safe handling is performed based on institutional guidelines.
Turn on incubator and set to 60°C
Gently remove coverslip from slide We recommend using a sterile edge blade to carefully remove coverslip. When handling sharps, ensure proper PPE and safe disposal.
Wash tissue with 1X PBS-T X3
Incubate tissue in βME stripping solution at 60°C for 45 min We recommend using glass staining jars for stripping incubation.
Wash tissue with Milli-Q H2O on shaker for 1 hour with water changes every 15 minutes
Wash tissue with 1X PBS-T X3
Incubate tissue with 1X TBS-T for 5 min
Wash tissue with 1X PBS-T X3
Incubate tissue with 1X TrueBlack for 5 min
Wash tissue with 1X PBS-T X3
Mount tissue with ProLong Glass
Coverslip slide
Confirm proper antibody elution under the microscope
Gently remove coverslip from slide
Wash tissue sections with 1X PBS-T X3
Block tissue with 5% NDS for 30 min
Start new round of staining
creator: Tsering Lama
